title: Chapter Eighty: The Full Moon
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

Today was the day.

He had done enough recuperation. Energy circulation to refine his body's interior -- finished. Now he needed to move.

The moment day broke, he requested guidance sparring from Cheong Myeong.

Sunlight filling the back of Radiant Demon Wing hall wrapped around the noble swordsman's tall frame. Blue Eyed Sword Demon. They said he contended for Radiant Demon Wing's strongest swordsman. Words circulated that he could be discussed as Desolate Fortress's number one blue rank -- and that included veteran masters.

CLANG.

He received Cheong Myeong's one-strike sword. Fierce wind struck his ears. The energy release was so deep that sword force climbed through the grip with vibrations that felt like a second blow.

The force was not unfamiliar. It was familiar. Between missions, Yeon-shin had received focused attention from powerful seniors -- Ma Jin, Cheong Myeong, Baek Mi-ryeo. The end of cultivation was martial matches. Losing was daily life. That was how it was in the main fortress's Radiant Demon Wing.

Perhaps because of this, today he was especially lax.

*Small piece.*

His mind drifted. The sun was setting but his thoughts were a thousand li away. His body had opened the middle dantian -- he should not tire even sparring all day. Yet focus slipped from him like water through cupped fingers.

During the exchange, the true qi in his grip grew loose. Intent completely relaxed.

SWOOSH.

Fierce blade wind. Shock through the sword body.

Clang.

The Desolate Sword flew from his hand. If he had not released it properly in that instant, his grip would have torn.

"Our Lightning Genius isn't in his right mind," Cheong Myeong said with a grin. He rested his sword on his shoulder. His smiling face made the words sting more. "Weren't you trying to enter a needle's hole? Quickly, that is."

*A scarier appearance than glaring.*

"You should aim for about three years to become Divine Sword Squad Leader. You have to spend half the remaining years building merits." The grin faded. Cold blue light spread from his single eye. "The current Divine Sword Squad Leader is very healthy. I can't imagine him losing no matter who he faces. Whether Thirteen Evil Sect lords or Eight Families leaders. Our Lightning Genius must defeat such a person."

"I'm sorry. When you're taking the trouble to guide me."

"Black rank is already an issue. The realm of internal energy cultivation itself must change -- this is the domain of enlightenment rather than talent." He tapped the sword blade on his shoulder. "Three Flowers Gathered at Crown, Five Qi Return to Source. The world of intent is different. Some dramatically call it shedding. They say it's hard to reach without firmly maintaining one's mind."

The tone was light. The content was not. Care was embedded in every syllable. In this moment, Cheong Myeong was picturing a more concrete future than Yeon-shin himself.

*Shameful.* Even more so because Cheong Myeong was the person Yeon-shin had grown closest to, along with Won-chang.

"Thank you for the advice. I only keep receiving."

"Enough with the formalities." Cheong Myeong sheathed his sword in a single elegant motion -- the blade drawing a half-moon of light. "Now it's better to set and study. My sword techniques, Mi-ryeo's energy techniques. Putting aside our leader who's such a busy person." He paused. "Hey. Why are you clasping hands again? Stop with the greetings."

Yeon-shin almost smiled. Almost.

"You should go to the Fortress Lord soon," Cheong Myeong said, his gaze drifting toward the roofline. "I'm dying of curiosity about what's happening too. Go quickly."

Yeon-shin looked where he looked. The white moon was rising over the dark grey roof. The full moon. Strangely distant tonight, as if it knew the weight of what it carried.

"I'll be going!"

The words came out louder than intended. He was not even embarrassed.

---

The night seeping into the air gently froze the boy's body. Dark and cosy, the way winter nights feel to those with somewhere warm to go.

The full moon welled up -- whitish, hazily smiling.

Yeon-shin entered the inner castle. Pure white marble. The gate guards gave only eye greetings. No one blocked him.

He moved in silence. The cold marble under his feet. The high stairs -- especially long tonight.

Still, still, there was an end.

The stone doors opened by themselves.

The opposite side, without walls, was wide open. The moon's pale light poured in unbroken. He raised his gaze to the tree trunk extending through the office, thick and ancient.

She was there.

Standing on a branch, receiving moonlight. Her glossy grass-coloured hair swaying long. Not a trace of energy waves could be felt, and yet she was clear. Distinct. The most present thing in the world.

The moonlight itself seemed to rush toward her. And Yeon-shin realised -- that was energy circulation. True qi operation at the scale of miracles. The full moon breathing with light's mist, streams refracting in eight directions to gather at her body.

*Even basic techniques have reached the realm of the impossible.*

"Though you knew what the piece was," she said without turning, "you must have been curious."

Her voice was the only sound beneath the moon's halo.

"The World Tree's fruit. Your guess was correct."

She turned.

Though she had her back to the moon, her eyes were visible first. Deep green. Distant. A feeling like naturally pressing down even the soul.

*A sensation even the Blood Flame Sect Leader never gave.*

"Come here."

"I greet the Fortress Lord."

He moved. Stepped up on the rough tree. Straightened his waist. Faced her.

"Closer."

"Yes."

Less than one step's distance now. The absolute being's face -- white enough to be transparent -- filled his vision.

He was about to bow and prostrate himself when she spoke.

"This is the first time since the evil sect's headquarters. There are words I must convey first."

He stared.

"I will listen attentively."

"The evil sect's leader may return."

The words struck him like ice water. Like a blade edge laid against the throat.

"It's supernatural power beyond reason," she said. "He regained his form with just one drop of blood. Though I ended his breath many times."

*The Blood Flame Sect Leader. Alive.*

He had thought the Fortress Lord had destroyed the enemy completely. She was saying she had not.

"I couldn't completely destroy him. I had to turn back to watch over you."

The math did not add up. One of the Thirteen Evil Sects' lords -- let go to keep a single rising disciple safe.

"I'm sorry," Yeon-shin said. He bowed his head. The memory of his battered body trying to face the Sixth Apostle burned behind his eyes.

"Sorry, you say." A crystalline voice. "It was my choice. You just focus on achievement while being wary of powerful enemies."

A pause.

"More than that."

Her gaze descended. He felt it -- the weight of her attention like the weight of the sky. He raised his head immediately.

Several strands of hair that had grown during recuperation rested on the Fortress Lord's hand. She was touching them with the absent curiosity of someone remembering something very old.

"In my youth, I made friends with World Tree leaves fallen in spring wind. Though now they are crumbled and gone." The corners of her mouth rose -- barely. "Your hair feels similar to that."

"Pardon?"

"When you become a master, it might be good to grow it out."

"Ah..."

He answered reflexively that he would. Her lips drew a small and beautiful curve.

*To the Fortress Lord, even blue rank isn't a master.*

But it was natural. No issue to make of it. It was the Fortress Lord's jest -- the first he had ever heard from her -- and it made him think she was human after all.

He stood on the ancient tree, in the moonlight, before the most powerful being he had ever known. The World Tree's fruit. The cure he had fought for, bled for, sacrificed everything for. It was here. It was real.

*Perhaps I could hope for a slightly bigger piece.*

The full moon hung above them, brilliant and round, and the light that poured through the open wall cast no shadows at all.
